Appropedia: Open-Source Atmospheric Water and Fog Harvesting Manuals
ScienceDirect: Aerodynamic Optimization and Yield Efficiency of Mesh Fog Collectors

technical_documents.description: An off-grid, large-scale atmospheric water-sovereignty infrastructure designed for hyper-arid coastal environments. Utilizing an aerodynamic, hydrophobic double-layer polymer mesh matrix, this system captures non-precipitating airborne moisture micro-droplets, converting dense coastal fog into high-yield, gravity-fed potable water streams without active operational energy requirements.
technical_documents.problem_statement: Coastal arid communities (e.g., Atacama, Namibia) suffer from extreme physical water scarcity, receiving under 20mm of annual rainfall while concurrently experiencing over 180 high-density advection fog days per year. Lacking centralized utilities, residents—primarily women and children—are forced to walk over 6+ km daily to retrieve poor-quality water from depleting, distant groundwater wells.
technical_documents.engineering_solution: The system deploys a high-tension 4m x 4m double-layer Raschel-woven polyethylene mesh grid characterized by an optimized 35% shade coefficient, anchored perpendicular to prevailing wind vectors. As moisture-laden fog envelopes travel through the mesh, suspended micro-droplets (1–40 μm) coalesce onto the hydrophobic monofilament fibers via fluid inertial impaction mechanics. Overcoming boundary layer drag, the growing drops cascade downwards along the vertical matrix under gravitational pull, draining into a 2% sloped collection gutter. This passive kinetic fluid cycle delivers a high-efficiency yield of 8–12 L/m²/day of mineral-clean freshwater directly into a sealed 200L cistern using zero electricity, active pumping, or external supply links.

bill_of_materials.name: UV-Stabilized Hydrophobic Raschel Polyethylene Mesh Netting (4m x 8m, Double-Layer Matrix)
Heavy-Duty Structural Wooden Support Poles (3m Length, x4) & Braided High-Tension Guy Ropes
Rigid PVC Airway-Sloped Collection Gutter (4m) & Downpipe Drainage Fittings
Food-Grade HDPE Water Storage Cistern (200L Capacity) with Hermetic Sealing Lid
Pre-Cast Concrete Anchor Blocks & Structural Steel Tensioning Turnbuckles (x4)

specifications.yield_capacity: 8–12 L/m²/day via fluid inertial impaction condensation

step_by_step_build: Step 1: Survey site for prevailing wind direction; position the collector perpendicular to dominant fog-bearing wind (typically onshore).
Step 2: Install four 3m poles in concrete footings; tension guy ropes for stability in wind.
Step 3: Stretch the double-layer mesh between poles; maintain 10cm gap between layers for droplet drainage.
Step 4: Mount PVC gutter at the mesh bottom edge sloping 2% toward the collection tank.
Step 5: Cover the tank with a lid to prevent evaporation and mosquito breeding; clean mesh monthly with mild vinegar solution.

comments.text: Harvesting 8–10 litres per day on the Chilean coast. Enough for a family of 4.
